prioritized_fixes.defect_class: faithfulness-form: non-substantive page-furniture (chrome) that verbatim-self-entails passes strict_verify and renders as a finding (I-wire-013 blind-predicate recurrence on 4 NEW furniture vocabularies: service-offline notice, OpenAlex record scaffold, 'Name - Honorific' byline, 'Publication date:/NN:N' masthead)
prioritized_fixes.change_summary: Add ONE new detector-only helper _contains_portal_masthead_status_chrome(text) next to the other _contains_* helpers and call it inside _contains_forensic_chrome just before the final return (~:2577). Four high-precision structure-anchored rules, each reusing _TRAILING_CITE_STRIP_RE + the _has_attributed_cited_finding KEEP guard: (1) service/dead-fetch interstitial (_SERVICE_OFFLINE_RE, fires only when no attributed cited finding survives); (2) OpenAlex portal record scaffold ('DetailsLocations', 'Year:/Type: article/Abstract:' ladder); (3) whole-unit ^Name [-] Honorific$ byline stub anchored with the honorific as terminal token; (4) masthead 'Publication date:' + bare NN:N volume:issue as a DUAL-signal with a (?<!the ) negative lookbehind. ~45 LOC, one file. Inherits render_chrome_screen_enabled() gate + FLAG-not-drop contract automatically. All five downstream consumers (body seam, corroboration ledger, key-findings, corroboration member, summary_table) route through the same predicate so this one addition closes every surface.

prioritized_fixes.defect_class: faithfulness-form: mid-word/mid-sentence truncated spans cited as findings (deterministic sub-forms) — trailing lone CAPITAL after a preposition ('...within C') and trailing bare COPULA/aux with value cut ('...pedicurists is')
prioritized_fixes.change_summary: In is_truncated_fragment add two module-level frozensets (_TRAILING_COPULA_AUX_CUT, _PREP_ARTICLE_BEFORE_LONE_LETTER) + a _ends_with_terminal_punct helper, and two new legs inside the existing `if core:` block. Copula/aux leg (fixes 'is'): inside the `not raw_last.isupper()` guard, flag when no terminal punctuation AND raw_last.lower() in _TRAILING_COPULA_AUX_CUT — overrides the _SHORT_OK_BOUNDARY_TOKENS whitelist for the unpunctuated case only. Lone-letter-after-connective leg (fixes 'within C'): its OWN block that runs even when raw_last.isupper(); flag when no terminal punctuation AND last token len==1 AND prev token in _PREP_ARTICLE_BEFORE_LONE_LETTER, so 'vitamin C'/'type A'/'grade B' (prev is a content noun) survive. This is the ONE predicate every render caller shares, so the body seam + both rollup upstreams are fixed at once, even with known_words=None. Validates fully OFFLINE (deterministic).

prioritized_fixes.defect_class: faithfulness-form: truncation semantic-gap sub-form — period-terminated but predicate-missing clause ('...over 90% of employees in China.') that surface trailing-token rules provably cannot catch
prioritized_fixes.change_summary: Two edits. (i) Enable + wire the LLM completeness judge screen_finding_units on the body finding seam: turn PG_SPAN_QUALITY_GATE on for the cert slate and extend the rollup wiring at run_honest_sweep_r3.py:16217-16220 to also screen body finding units, still FLAG-NOT-DROP (withheld from summary/finding surface, retained in body/evidence). (ii) Fix the precision-narrowing contradiction in _apply_precision_narrowing truncation branch (:376-383): today it demotes a judge 'truncation' verdict back to clean whenever _ends_complete_sentence is True, which un-flags every period-terminated clause-cut. Change it to STOP demoting on the ends-complete signal alone — demote ONLY the deterministically-separable FP shape (a trailing multi-citation cluster on an otherwise well-formed sentence) and otherwise DEFER to the judge verdict, as the module docstring already promises. This is the ONLY fix touching span_quality_gate.py — no overlap with other fixes. Needs a fresh paid run to fully validate (LLM path).

prioritized_fixes.defect_class: faithfulness-form: aspect-blind topicality — same-entity/wrong-sub-domain source (GenAI entity satisfied, labor-market aspect absent) passes both off-topic judges because they score ENTITY match, not the question's ASPECT (source [21] student-dishonesty-in-L2-writing grounds the Abstract of a labor-market question)
prioritized_fixes.change_summary: PRIMARY: rewrite the entity-level instruction block in _build_batch_prompt (:181-186) into a two-part facet-scoped rubric (domain-agnostic, no hardcoded aspect per LAW VI): instruct the model to first name the question's SUBJECT ENTITY and its SPECIFIC ASPECT, mark ON only if the source bears on BOTH, mark same-entity/different-aspect as OFF ('same subject, wrong question'), with a domain-neutral one-shot exemplar, and preserve fail-open explicitly ('if you genuinely cannot tell the aspect is addressed, answer ON'). Keep the exact `<index>: ON|OFF` output contract (parser unchanged). This flips [21] to OFF -> the UNCHANGED default demote path stamps topic_offtopic_demoted -> _is_confirmed_offtopic returns True -> the existing DEFER-1 cite-suppression, section-assignment hold-out, and F3 compose-demote all engage; source stays in evidence_pool + off-topic disclosure (demote+disclose, no hard-drop). REINFORCING (same PR, cheap): extend _CONTENT_RELEVANCE_PROMPT INSUFFICIENT (src/polaris_graph/generator/relevance_judge.py:247-250) to explicitly cover same-entity/different-aspect, onto the existing INSUFFICIENT->demote weight. Do NOT touch the reranker numeric band (that is the §-1.3-banned knob). Note stale test test_scope_filter_iscope001.py:189 asserts topic_gate_enabled() False vs shipped default ON — reconcile when the fix lands.

prioritized_fixes.defect_class: faithfulness-form: explicit user-stated temporal bound ('published before June 2023') not enforced because the enforcement predicate is starved of per-source publication dates for every non-OpenAlex source (Part A: pub-date resolution)
prioritized_fixes.change_summary: New module publication_date_resolver.py exposing resolve_publication_date(raw_html, jsonld, url, pdf_meta) -> (iso_ym, year) running an htmldate-style cascade over ALREADY-fetched content (zero new network): JSON-LD datePublished from the existing _extract_jsonld_blocks output; meta article:published_time / citation_publication_date / DC.date.issued / prism.publicationDate; microdata itemprop=datePublished; <time datetime>; URL /YYYY/MM/; PDF /CreationDate. Accept only 1900<=year<=2100; treat low-confidence body-heuristic dates as undated (avoid mis-dating an in-window source). Wire in live_retriever.py immediately after :5818 guarded ONLY for the OpenAlex-miss case (`if _pub_date is None and _pub_year is None and enabled: ...`). The existing :6227-6234 block then stamps pub_date/publication_year/year, so constraint_enforcement._row_pub_ym AND evidence_selector._row_year immediately start seeing dates on web/institutional rows — no downstream change. Env gate PG_RESOLVE_PUBDATE_FROM_HTML (LAW VI). Pure provenance metadata; never enters a verified claim. NOTE the OpenAlex 'Year: 2026' [12] survival is a secondary seam (row-repr loss / try-except fail-open / basket-render path not gated) — confirm against the run's scope_grounding_excluded_selection.json before closing.

prioritized_fixes.defect_class: faithfulness-form: temporal-bound enforcement policy for rows still undated after resolution (Part B: hard-mask vs keep-disclose under an explicit stated ceiling) — carries an operator decision
prioritized_fixes.change_summary: Add env knob PG_TIMELINE_HARD_UNDATED_POLICY read in build_scope_enforcement with three values: keep_disclose (current fail-open, default) | mask (fail-closed: under a HARD stated bound an undated row is treated out-of-window, added to grounding_excluded_ids + disclosed) | resolve_then_mask (mask only rows still undated after FF5). In the timeline block (:308-322), when timeline_hard_active and policy in {mask,resolve_then_mask} and _row_pub_ym(row) is None, add url to grounding_excluded_ids with action 'timeline_hard_undated_excluded_disclosed'. Reuses the already-blessed I-scope-001 HARD grounding-mask seam; masked source stays in the pool + disclosure (WEIGHT-don't-FILTER). Fires ONLY under an explicit extractor-detected requirement clause — scope-compliance, not a breadth cap. DO NOT ship the mask default until the operator signs the hard-exclude-vs-down-weight question; recommended landing is default keep_disclose, operator flips to resolve_then_mask after FF5.

prioritized_fixes.defect_class: faithfulness-form: internal-consistency defect — Methods/Limitations states horizon 'through June 2023' while post-window sources are cited (Part C: derive disclosure from actual grounded span, not the parroted protocol bound)
prioritized_fixes.change_summary: Derive the Methods/Limitations horizon sentence from the ACTUAL grounded-source date span plus the scope_timeline_disclosure.json enforcement counts (masked N, undated-kept M), not from a parroted protocol date_range. Separate composer/render change — flag but DO NOT bundle with FF5/FF6. Lower priority than the resolution + policy fixes; lands after FF5 so the grounded date span is populated.

coverage_track:
COVERAGE/DEPTH is its OWN co-equal workstream, stood up from the start as a separate gated track — NOT derived from the defect list (per feedback_defect_audit_blind_to_coverage_2026_07_05: a defect audit finds presence-of-wrong and is structurally blind to absence-of-right). It is WIDEN-only per §-1.3: every arm RAISES a budget or WIDENS candidate recall; the one hard gate (strict_verify / NLI entailment / 4-role D8 / provenance span-grounding) stays byte-untouched; every widened source is re-verified before it can cite and every widened pair/cluster is still licensed by the certified consolidation_nli/edge engine and must still carry >=2 distinct cited evidence_ids. No cap/target/thinner is added; nothing forces a breadth number. Symptoms to close: retrieval funnel 166->151->81->27, depth pre-pass '0/3 baskets drafted', 'baskets_total=65 drafted=0 kept=1 (cross=0)', final Reliability 'multi-source corroborated (>=2 verified origins): 0', GenAI-RCT core (Noy&Zhang, Peng, Felten) absent, Brynjolfsson [7] retrieved but strict-verify-dropped.

THREE ARMS.

Arm A - surface the empirical core (retrieval widen + citation snowball). New module src/polaris_graph/retrieval/landmark_study_expander.py mirroring sub_entity_query_expander: plan_landmark_study_queries(question, llm) fires ONE bounded LOCKED-generator call ('list the landmark empirical studies/RCTs/seminal datasets, with author teams, most central to answering <question>; one scope-anchored web query per line'); widen_with_landmark_studies RAISES the query budget as a byte-SUPERSET (never swaps a baseline query). Wire into fs_researcher_query_gen._plan_expert_facet_queries right after the sub-entity block (~:408); gate PG_LANDMARK_STUDY_EXPANSION default ON; also flip PG_SUBENTITY_QUERY_EXPANSION ON in the cert slate. PLUS citation snowball: snowball_citation_neighbors(doi/paperId) in frame_fetcher pulls forward+backward neighbors from Semantic Scholar graph + OpenAlex referenced_works/cited_by, fetched top-k through the SAME seed_only live-retrieval chokepoint (gate PG_CITATION_SNOWBALL default ON, bounded per seed). This reliably co-cites Noy&Zhang/Peng/Felten off Brynjolfsson/Eloundou. Every discovered source flows the UNCHANGED per_query_retrieve + frozen verify — never auto-trusted.

Arm B - recover the DOI-only landmark primary (Brynjolfsson). In frame_fetcher._fetch_frame_entity_inner, when DOI-Unpaywall/best_oa_location returns closed, add an OA-preprint discovery fallback BEFORE the METADATA_ONLY/ABSTRACT_ONLY fall-through: (i) read OpenAlex work.locations / S2 openAccessPdf for ANY host copy (returns the NBER w31161 / arXiv / RePEc PDF even when the journal DOI is closed); else (ii) bounded Serper '<title>' site:nber.org OR arxiv.org OR ssrn.com OR repec.org; fetch the discovered PDF through the existing CORE/AccessBypass oa_full_text path (:1495-1521) -> _pick_richest_abstract usability guard. Combine with M3 FIX-1 (carry doi/pmid into the bib row) + FIX-2 (gather-richest-abstract + S2). Gate PG_FRAME_OA_PREPRINT_RECOVERY default ON. Inject as ordinary corpus rows with their REAL fetched URL — never relabel a preprint as the paywalled DOI's canonical record. A primary with genuinely no OA copy STAYS a disclosed gap; strict_verify simply receives real verbatim RCT-number spans to ground.

Arm C - widen cross-source PAIRING recall, keep NLI as the gate (unblocks the analytical layer; C1 is the LEAD authorable edit, the single-function tightest unblock of the '0 cross-source' symptom). C1: in cross_source_synthesis.compose_cross_source_analytical_units replace the exact subject|predicate anchor bucketing (:521-534) with generate_pair_candidates(baskets) pairing baskets that are (i) embedding-similar (resident Qwen3 embedder cosine >= PG_CROSS_SOURCE_PAIR_SIM ~0.62, zero new spend) OR (ii) share a named entity / numeric quantity. UNCHANGED downstream: each candidate still decided by license_relation + _cross_source_relation_signals (certified NLI/edge), still needs >=2 distinct cited evidence_ids and re-verify; make the fail-loud canary fire on candidate_pairs>0 too. Gate PG_CROSS_SOURCE_PAIR_RECALL default ON. C2: in the clustering that feeds credibility_pass baskets add an agglomerative embed-cosine merge CONFIRMED by consolidation_nli BIDIRECTIONAL entailment (refuse non-mutually-entailing merges) so genuine same-finding corroborators land in ONE basket and more baskets clear the >=2-origin floor; keep the >=2 floor and per-member isolated verify EXACTLY as-is (a wrong over-merge, the clinical-lethal direction, is refused by the bidirectional NLI). Gate PG_CONSOLIDATION_EMBED_RECALL default ON.

CO-EQUAL GATING + SAFE/AGGRESSIVE PARALLEL: run TWO configs in parallel on the fresh small real run per the operator rule - a SAFE config (Arms A+B only, conservative sim threshold, small snowball k) and an AGGRESSIVE config (all arms, sim ~0.60, larger snowball k) - read each against a 5-row PROVEN/NOT coverage table: (a) corpus mentions Noy&Zhang/Peng/Felten, (b) Brynjolfsson [7] renders a verified span, (c) baskets with >=2 verified origins > 3, (d) D8-thread cross>0, (e) final 'multi-source corroborated' > 0. Land the wideners ONE flag at a time so each effect is attributable in a fresh run (turning all on at once makes attribution impossible; a low sim floods the NLI with junk candidates - a compute cost, not a faithfulness risk, since the NLI still refuses bad merges). Every arm dual-gated Codex+Fable before merge. Keep every widener scope-anchored (game-rule official-question mode) so it widens the empirical core, not off-topic breadth.
judge_infra_fix:
The D8 4-role Judge (moonshotai/kimi-k2.6) returns a blank verdict under load because _judge_reasoning_block (src/polaris_graph/roles/openrouter_role_transport.py:364) hands kimi a BARE unbounded reasoning block {enabled: True} with NO reasoning.max_tokens cap, against a top-level max_tokens of only 16384. OpenRouter counts reasoning tokens as OUTPUT tokens drawn from that same budget, so a deliberative pass that reasons past ~16k emits ZERO content -> the observed #1026 'blank verdict with bare reasoning'. The numeric reasoning cap that prevents exactly this (reasoning_cap_for / build_judge_reasoning_block in src/polaris_graph/llm/judge_reasoning_block.py:96,136) is applied to GLM-family slugs ONLY; the kimi Judge falls through UNCAPPED.

FAIL-CLOSED IS ALREADY CORRECT AND MUST BE PRESERVED UNTOUCHED: on ladder exhaustion the transport raises BlankVerdictError, sweep_integration.future.result() (:701-702) propagates it, and with PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEGRADE on the claim consumes as an UNGROUNDED degrade that _compose_final_verdict downgrades (never upgraded/approved). So a blank ALREADY fails closed - the surviving defect is a latency/liveness STALL that blocks the final manifest + DeepTRACE scoring, NOT a faithfulness hole. Do NOT touch the blank->UNGROUNDED path.

PRIMARY fix (surgical, faithfulness-neutral): reuse the proven cap. Change _judge_reasoning_block to also accept the resolved verdict budget and, for a kimi slug, return {enabled: True, max_tokens: reasoning_cap_for(max_tokens)} (reasoning_cap_for(16384) ~= 12384, strictly below 16384, reserving the >=4000 content floor) so the verdict JSON always has room and the starvation blank cannot occur. Keep top-level max_tokens UNCHANGED at 16384 (:1449) so all 21 kimi endpoints (DeepInfra 16384 floor) stay in OpenRouter's load-balance - NO provider narrowing. Thread the budget in at the two call sites that already know it: :1347 (build) and :1812 (retry rung). reasoning.max_tokens and reasoning.effort are mutually exclusive, which is fine because kimi carries no effort. GUARD (read the API, don't guess): before shipping, live GET /models + /endpoints for moonshotai/kimi-k2.6 to confirm the endpoints accept reasoning.max_tokens without a 400; for any subset that rejects it, keep the bare block for those endpoints and lean on the throttle+liveness levers.

SECONDARY levers (tune existing knobs, add nothing): (a) seed a lower steady judge concurrency for account-shared-rate all-GLM runs (PG_FOUR_ROLE_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY=2) so the judge stops feeding the account-wide 429 storm; the AIMD controller is already on. (b) set a judge-specific TIGHTER per-call liveness wall (lower PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_TOTAL_S_JUDGE_FLOOR from 1800 toward measured p99, e.g. 300-600, mirroring the Sentinel 300s / entailment 150s fail-fast) so the already-ON force-close rotation abandons a hung/trickling congested provider in minutes-not-tens-of-minutes - size to MEASURED p99, not a guess (no persisted judge latency samples yet; measure in the preflight small run first). (c) OPTIONAL belt-and-suspenders: JSON-schema response_format constrained decoding masks an empty content field at generation - gate behind a flag since response_format support varies by provider and can narrow the spread.

BLANK MUST FAIL CLOSED: the fix only reshapes the transport TOKEN BUDGET (splitting 16384 into bounded reasoning + guaranteed content floor) and how-many/how-fast judge POSTs run; no verification threshold moves; any residual blank still under-credits (disclosed gap), never over-credits. Preflight per operator rule: a SMALL real run (single question, all-GLM lineup with the kimi judge) must complete the final scoring pass with zero #1026 rotate-warnings and no >5-min no-log-line gap before the large paid run. Dual-gate Codex+Fable before merge. The lock file still pins judge=qwen; the kimi default lives only on the transport benchmark path (PG_BENCHMARK_JUDGE_MODEL) - the fix must stay on the benchmark path and NOT alter the qwen lock or trip verify_lock/canonical-pin.
sequencing:
BUILD FIRST, IN PARALLEL (all touch DISTINCT files, no cross-dependency, land as separate diffs): FF1-CHROME (weighted_enrichment.py _contains_forensic_chrome), FF2-TRUNC-DET (key_findings.py is_truncated_fragment), FF4-ASPECT (topic_relevance_gate.py prompt + relevance_judge.py INSUFFICIENT), FF5-DATE-RESOLVE (new publication_date_resolver.py + live_retriever wiring), the JUDGE-INFRA primary (openrouter_role_transport.py _judge_reasoning_block reusing reasoning_cap_for), and the COVERAGE Arm C1 lead edit (cross_source_synthesis.py) - the tightest unblock of the 0-cross-source symptom. These six parallelize cleanly.

DEDUP WATCH: FF1-CHROME and FF2-TRUNC-DET both feed the ONE shared render predicate is_render_chrome_or_unrenderable, but through DIFFERENT helpers in DIFFERENT files (_contains_forensic_chrome in weighted_enrichment.py vs is_truncated_fragment in key_findings.py) - they COMPOSE, no merge conflict, but run the combined render-chrome regression suite (test_render_chrome_prose_screen.py, test_p1_chrome_gate_box1.py, test_deepfix_chrome_canary_unblind.py) AFTER BOTH land to confirm zero cross-interaction false-positives. FF3-TRUNC-SEM is the ONLY fix touching span_quality_gate.py - no overlap. FF6/FF7 are the only two touching constraint_enforcement.py / multi_section_generator.py for the date defect - no overlap with the coverage arms.

SECOND WAVE (each depends on a first-wave fix): FF3-TRUNC-SEM after FF2-TRUNC-DET (same defect, shared tests; also needs PG_SPAN_QUALITY_GATE turned on for the cert slate); FF6-DATE-POLICY after FF5-DATE-RESOLVE (resolve_then_mask needs Part A; carries the operator hard-exclude-vs-down-weight decision - land default keep_disclose, do NOT ship the mask default until the operator signs); FF7-DATE-DISCLOSE after FF5-DATE-RESOLVE (separate composer PR, do not bundle); COVERAGE Arms A + B and C2 after C1 lands (one flag at a time).

WHAT MUST GATE BEFORE RE-SMOKE: EVERY fix passes the DUAL real-model gate - the real Codex CLI (verdict parsed from the WRITTEN file, never a self-report) AND real Claude Fable 5 - BOTH APPROVE, per fix, before merge (feedback_dual_gate_real_codex_real_fable5_keep). Then the WHOLE wave gates together before the paid fresh run.

CRITICAL VALIDATION RULE (feedback_offline_tests_not_real_preflight + banked-replay-blind): the chrome (FF1), truncation-render (FF2/FF3), date-fetch (FF5), and coverage-fetch arms CANNOT be validated by a banked replay - the banked spans are pre-fix truncated/chrome and the fetch-side changes never fire on a replay. Only a FRESH front-half small real drb_72 run validates them, on the VM (never local). So: land + dual-gate all diffs with offline RED/GREEN tests -> ONE small FRESH real run with a per-fix PROVEN/NOT table (the 4 chrome strings absent, the 3 truncation forms absent, [21] off-aspect off the Abstract, no post-June-2023 grounded citation, the 5-row coverage table with Brynjolfsson verified + cross>0, zero judge #1026) -> only then the large paid beat-both run. Run the coverage SAFE and AGGRESSIVE configs in parallel on that fresh run (co-equal-track rule). Green offline is LOGIC only, not live-wiring - do NOT declare victory on a green suite.
open_questions_for_operator: Date-window undated-row policy (FF6-DATE-POLICY): for a source under an EXPLICIT user-stated ceiling ('published before June 2023') whose publication date cannot be resolved even after FF5's HTML/PDF extraction, do we HARD-EXCLUDE it from answer grounding (fail-closed mask, disclosed) or DOWN-WEIGHT + disclose it (keep_disclose, current fail-open)? Recommended default: keep_disclose until FF5 lands, then flip to resolve_then_mask (mask only rows still undated after resolution) - this is the maximally scope-compliant setting with minimal false-masking. This fires ONLY under an explicit stated bound and every excluded row stays in the pool + disclosure, so it is scope-compliance, not a §-1.3 breadth cap - but the hard-exclude vs down-weight choice is yours to sign.

D8 Judge budget (JUDGE-INFRA): keep moonshotai/kimi-k2.6 and BOUND its reasoning below the current top-level max_tokens=16384 (RECOMMENDED - preserves all 21 kimi providers and the 429 resilience the kimi swap was chosen for), OR raise PG_D8_VERDICT_MAX_TOKENS toward kimi's real 262144 cap (more raw headroom but OpenRouter FILTERS the judge to the high-cap provider subset, dropping DeepInfra 16384 and re-opening the 429/blank render-blocker - the transport comment records the prior 262140 raise causing 193x 429 + 67x 400), OR swap the judge to an even-higher-provider-count open reasoner (lock-touching: canonical_pin + family guard + operator signature, out of scope for a surgical fix). Options (a) and (b) pull in OPPOSITE directions on provider count.
